repos.name: distributed-gateway-order-system

pinnedRepos.name: NextJS_EspacoGeek
pinnedRepos.description: The modern, responsive frontend application for the Espaço Geek platform, built with Next.js and React. It consumes a robust GraphQL API via Apollo Client and features a dynamic UI styled with Tailwind CSS, built-in internationalization (i18n), JWT-based authentication, and user profile management for tracking geek culture media (Anime, Movies etc)

pinnedRepos.name: SpringBoot-JobApplyTracker
pinnedRepos.description: ⚙️ Robust REST API for the JobApplyTracker system. Built with Spring Boot and MariaDB, featuring JWT authentication, Flyway migrations, and comprehensive monitoring with Prometheus & Grafana.

pinnedRepos.name: React-JobApplyTracker
pinnedRepos.description: 🚀 A modern, responsive frontend application to track and manage job applications. Built with React, Vite, and Tailwind CSS, it features a comprehensive dashboard, application status tracking, and authentication.

issue.title: 05 - Implement session join flow with temporary session code
issue.body:
## Context

The Flutter viewer joins a stream session using a temporary code generated by the Windows publisher through the backend.

Related repositories:

- Backend: https://github.com/vitorhugo-java/dotnet_SonicRelay
- Windows publisher: https://github.com/vitorhugo-java/windows_SonicRelay

Expected backend endpoint:

```text
POST /api/sessions/join
```

## Codex prompt

```text
You are working on the SonicRelay Flutter mobile viewer.

Goal:
Implement the session join flow using a temporary session code.

Hard requirements:
- Keep Feature Driven Development structure.
- Implement session logic inside lib/features/sessions.
- Use the authenticated Dio client from core/http.
- Require a registered Flutter viewer device id before joining.
- Normalize session code input to uppercase.
- Validate empty and invalid-looking session codes before HTTP call.
- Keep UI logic in the ViewModel, not inside widgets.

Create or improve:
- lib/features/sessions/data/sessions_api.dart
- lib/features/sessions/data/sessions_repository.dart
- lib/features/sessions/data/dto/join_session_request.dart
- lib/features/sessions/data/dto/join_session_response.dart
- lib/features/sessions/domain/stream_session.dart
- lib/features/sessions/domain/session_status.dart
- lib/features/sessions/presentation/join_session_view_model.dart
- lib/features/sessions/presentation/join_session_page.dart
- lib/features/sessions/presentation/session_waiting_page.dart
- lib/features/sessions/presentation/widgets/session_code_input.dart
- lib/features/sessions/presentation/widgets/session_status_card.dart

Expected request shape:
{
  "code": "ABC123",
  "deviceId": "viewer_device_uuid"
}

Expected response shape:
{
  "sessionId": "session_uuid",
  "role": "viewer",
  "signalingUrl": "wss://stream.example.com/ws/signaling?sessionId=session_uuid"
}

Behavior:
1. User types the code shown by the Windows publisher.
2. App validates and normalizes the code.
3. App calls POST /api/sessions/join with code and deviceId.
4. App stores the returned session context in memory.
5. App navigates to a waiting/connecting page.
6. App prepares the signaling URL for the next issue.

Error handling:
- Invalid code: show friendly validation.
- Expired code: show clear message.
- Max viewers reached: show clear message.
- Network failure: show retry option.
- Unauthorized: redirect to login.

Testing:
- Unit test session code normalization.
- Unit test join success flow.
- Unit test expired/invalid/max-viewers errors.
- Widget test code input UI.

Acceptance criteria:
- flutter analyze passes.
- flutter test passes.
- Join session page uses the repository/view model.
- A valid response produces a session context for signaling.
- README documents the session join flow.
```

performed_via_github_app.name: ChatGPT Codex Connector
performed_via_github_app.description: Bring ChatGPT and Codex to your GitHub repositories.

comment.body: Implemented in commit 9d83e36. Added the authenticated session join flow, registered-device requirement, validation/error handling, in-memory signaling context, waiting UI, tests, and README documentation. Verified with flutter analyze and flutter test.

issue.title: 04 - Implement device registration and device listing for Flutter viewer
issue.body:
## Context

The backend models devices as user-owned clients. The Flutter mobile app must register itself as a `flutter_viewer` device and list user devices when needed.

Related repositories:

- Backend: https://github.com/vitorhugo-java/dotnet_SonicRelay
- Windows publisher: https://github.com/vitorhugo-java/windows_SonicRelay

Expected backend endpoints:

```text
POST   /api/devices
GET    /api/devices
GET    /api/devices/{deviceId}
PATCH  /api/devices/{deviceId}
DELETE /api/devices/{deviceId}
```

## Codex prompt

```text
You are working on the SonicRelay Flutter mobile viewer.

Goal:
Implement device registration and device listing for the Flutter viewer app.

Hard requirements:
- Keep Feature Driven Development structure.
- Implement device logic inside lib/features/devices.
- Use the authenticated Dio client from core/http.
- Register this mobile app as type flutter_viewer.
- Detect platform as android or ios where practical.
- Store the current device id through a repository abstraction.
- Do not duplicate auth token logic in this feature.
- Do not use the device name as an identifier.

Create or improve:
- lib/features/devices/data/devices_api.dart
- lib/features/devices/data/devices_repository.dart
- lib/features/devices/data/dto/register_device_request.dart
- lib/features/devices/data/dto/device_response.dart
- lib/features/devices/domain/device.dart
- lib/features/devices/domain/device_type.dart
- lib/features/devices/presentation/devices_view_model.dart
- lib/features/devices/presentation/widgets/device_card.dart

Behavior:
1. After login, ensure the current mobile device is registered.
2. If a device id already exists locally, validate or reuse it.
3. If there is no local device id, call POST /api/devices.
4. Store the returned device id.
5. Allow listing devices for debugging/settings UI.
6. Surface friendly errors when device registration fails.

Testing:
- Unit test device repository mapping.
- Unit test first registration flow.
- Unit test existing local device id flow.
- Widget test DeviceCard.

Acceptance criteria:
- flutter analyze passes.
- flutter test passes.
- Current Flutter app registers as flutter_viewer.
- Device id is available for session join and WebSocket signaling later.
- README documents device registration flow.
```

performed_via_github_app.name: ChatGPT Codex Connector
performed_via_github_app.description: Bring ChatGPT and Codex to your GitHub repositories.

comment.body: Implemented in e4fe521 on main. Added authenticated flutter_viewer registration, secure current-device ID persistence and validation, device listing in Settings, friendly failure state, README documentation, and focused repository/view-model/widget tests. Validation: flutter analyze (no issues) and flutter test (24 passed).
